items.text: 1. lume/cli/handlers.py becomes a lume/cli/handlers/ package, handlers grouped by concern: lifecycle.py (new, reopen, close, open, migrate, handle_transition), discovery.py (verbs, entities, schema, status, get + _ENTITY_KEY, snapshot), authoring.py (plan, decide, retro). (Evidence: the package + submodules exist; handlers.py is gone.)

2. handlers/__init__.py assembles the HANDLERS registry from the groups and re-exports HANDLERS + handle_transition, so app.py's `from .handlers import HANDLERS, handle_transition` is unchanged. (Evidence: app.py import untouched; HANDLERS covers the same verbs as before.)
3. Handler bodies are moved verbatim (no logic change); imports adjusted per submodule. No circular import. (Evidence: imports resolve; reading shows identical handler logic.)

4. Behaviour byte-for-byte identical; full suite passes with NO assertion edits; public surface (lume.cli.main/_CATALOG/_VERB_NAMES) + bin/lume unchanged. (Evidence: test run 0 failures; live spot-check.)
5. No new dependency; deterministic; each submodule is focused and importable on its own; no logic in handlers/__init__ beyond assembly + re-export. (Evidence: imports resolve.)

dod.preamble: P4 (added after reopen, per the closeout carry-forward): split the ~290-line handlers.py into a handlers/ package grouped by concern (lifecycle / discovery / authoring), with __init__ assembling the HANDLERS registry and re-exporting handle_transition so app's import is unchanged. Pure relocation of handler bodies - behaviour-preserving, guarded by the existing suite (green, no assertion edits).

handback:
P4 complete - handlers.py split into a lume/cli/handlers/ package by concern. 291 tests pass, no assertion edits, behaviour byte-identical.

- lifecycle.py (new/reopen/close/open/migrate/transition), discovery.py (verbs/entities/schema/status/get/snapshot), authoring.py (plan/decide/retro), __init__.py assembles HANDLERS + re-exports handle_transition.
- app.py import unchanged; 14 handlers; no circular import; bin/lume + public names intact.
- Handler bodies verbatim; largest group file is 131 lines.

This was added post-closeout per the carry-forward. Accept, then I'll re-close the workstream (it's the same objective; the 005 retro stands).
self_review:
All 5 DoD items complete; 291 tests pass with NO assertion edits; behaviour byte-identical (verbs --json=20, gate->1, reject usage->2 verified live).

handlers.py (~290 lines) is now a lume/cli/handlers/ package grouped by concern: lifecycle.py (82 - new/reopen/close/open/migrate/handle_transition), discovery.py (131 - verbs/entities/schema/status/get+_ENTITY_KEY/snapshot), authoring.py (70 - plan/decide/retro), __init__.py (48 - assembles HANDLERS from the groups + re-exports handle_transition). Handler bodies moved verbatim; only import depth changed (... for the grandparent package).

app.py's `from .handlers import HANDLERS, handle_transition` is unchanged (the package presents the same surface). 14 handlers registered, same as before. No circular import; public surface (lume.cli.main/_CATALOG/_VERB_NAMES) + bin/lume intact.

Caught a process slip: my first delete of handlers.py ran from the wrong cwd and left the stale module shadowed by the package; removed it (and its stale .pyc) and re-verified. Each handler group is now a focused <=130-line file.
